data.description:
**Describe the Parameter or Relation**
Stable gonality is missing

**Source**
Link to a book or published paper that establishes said results.
[For easy observation give a reason for the change, e.g., a short proof or counterexample.](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2202.06838)

(relation received by e-mail)

**Describe the Parameter or Relation**
carving-width is upper-bounded by the product of treewidth and max degree

In [1], this bound is attributed to [2].

**Source**
 [1] D. Marx, A. Sidiropoulos.
The limited blessing of low dimensionality: when 1-1/d is the best possible exponent for d-dimensional geometric problems.
In SoCG 2014, pages 67-76. https://doi.org/10.1145/2582112.2582124

[2] D. M. Thilikos, M. J. Serna, and H. L. Bodlaender.
Constructive linear time

vertex integrity is also sometimes known as star-width, i.e. I want a tree decomposition of the graph where the tree is a star. 

definition: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0020019017300704
but no relation to vi there..., 
the proof of equivalence is roughly as follows:
sw -> vi
the root of the decomposition is the separator to the vertex integrity
vi -> sw
the separator forms the root of the decomposition, everything else is maybe connected to it -> star-decomoposition

**Describe the Parameter or Relation**
treebandwidth

**Source**
defined in https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.11674v1

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Many parameters can be described through cops & robber (search) games. These games specify how cops move, how robber moves, and rules of the game (how turn progresses), how the parameter is defined through the game.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add definitions of the parameters through the games.

**Additional context**
Some games are mentioned in https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.11674v1

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Many parameters can be equivalently described through excluding a structure as a minor, or subgraph, or induced minor, or induced subgraph, etc.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Create parameters through excluding parametric graph classes via containment relations. Add relation inference of the derived parameters through relation of the graph classes and containment relation.

**Additional context**
Some parameter definition

**Describe the Parameter or Relation**
dipole number

**Source**
unsure

**Additional context**
mentioned in https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.11674v1 seems to be based on excluding $K_{2,l}$

**Describe the Parameter or Relation**
fan number

**Source**
unsure

**Additional context**
mentioned in https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.11674v1 seems to be based on excluding a fan

**Describe the Parameter or Relation**
(slim) tree-cut width

**Source**
https://drops.dagstuhl.de/storage/00lipics/lipics-vol249-ipec2022/LIPIcs.IPEC.2022.15/LIPIcs.IPEC.2022.15.pdf

I wanted to point you to this paper here:
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.11674v1
There's multiple things in there that could be interesting:
- They have a parameter relationship diagram in Figure 8 on page 36, where they relate multiple parameters which seem to be missing from HOPS (domino treewidth, (slim) tree-cut width, edge-treewidth, tree-partition-width, fan number, dipole number,...).
- They give a list of parametric obstructions for a lot of parameters in Table 1 of page 38, maybe such overv
